#' ---
#' title: "CHARLS Harmonized Dataset — Analytical Vignettes"
#' author: "aiver2001"
#' date: "2026-06-13"
#' output: html_document
#' ---
#'
#' # Example Analyses with the Harmonized CHARLS Dataset
#'
#' This vignette demonstrates common longitudinal analysis patterns
#' using the harmonized CHARLS panel dataset.
#'
#' ## Setup

# Load required packages
library(survival)
library(lme4)
library(geepack)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Load harmonized dataset (adjust path as needed)
charls <- read.csv("charls_harmonized.csv")
cat(sprintf("Loaded: %d individuals × %d variables\n", nrow(charls), ncol(charls)))

#' ---
#' ## Example 1: Association of Baseline Hypertension with All-Cause Mortality
#'
#' Using Cox proportional hazards with baseline (Wave 1, 2011) predictors.

# Prepare baseline data
baseline <- charls %>%
  select(ID, age_wave1, gender_wave1, rural_wave1, education_wave1,
         hibpe_wave1, diabe_wave1, hearte_wave1, stroke_wave1,
         smokev_wave1, bmi_wave1, DEAD, DEADYEAR) %>%
  mutate(
    gender = factor(gender_wave1, labels = c("Male", "Female")),
    rural  = factor(rural_wave1, labels = c("Urban", "Rural")),
    smoke  = factor(smokev_wave1, labels = c("Never", "Former", "Current")),
    surv_time = ifelse(DEAD == 1, DEADYEAR - 2011, 2020 - 2011)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(age_wave1))

# Fit Cox model
cox_fit <- coxph(Surv(surv_time, DEAD) ~ age_wave1 + gender + rural +
                  hibpe_wave1 + diabe_wave1 + hearte_wave1 + stroke_wave1 +
                  smoke + bmi_wave1, data = baseline)

summary(cox_fit)

#' ---
#' ## Example 2: Longitudinal Trajectory of CESD-10 Depression Scores
#'
#' Linear mixed-effects model for repeated CESD-10 measures across waves.

# Reshape to long format
cesd_long <- charls %>%
  select(ID, age_wave1, gender_wave1, rural_wave1,
         cesd10_wave1, cesd10_wave2, cesd10_wave3, cesd10_wave4, cesd10_wave5) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("cesd10_wave"),
    names_to = "wave",
    names_pattern = "cesd10_wave(.*)",
    values_to = "cesd10",
    values_drop_na = TRUE
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    wave = as.numeric(wave),
    gender = factor(gender_wave1, labels = c("Male", "Female")),
    rural  = factor(rural_wave1, labels = c("Urban", "Rural"))
  )

# Fit linear mixed model
lmm <- lmer(cesd10 ~ wave + age_wave1 + gender + rural + (1 | ID),
            data = cesd_long)

summary(lmm)

# Plot trajectories for 50 random individuals
set.seed(42)
rand_ids <- sample(unique(cesd_long$ID), 50)
cesd_long %>%
  filter(ID %in% rand_ids) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = wave, y = cesd10, group = ID)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "darkred", size = 1.5) +
  labs(x = "Wave", y = "CESD-10 Score",
       title = "CESD-10 Depression Trajectories (2011–2020)",
       subtitle = "50 randomly selected CHARLS participants") +
  theme_minimal()

#' ---
#' ## Example 3: GEE for Binary Outcome — Incident Hypertension
#'
#' Generalized Estimating Equations for hypertension incidence across waves.

# Identify wave 1 non-hypertensive participants, track incident hypertension
htn_long <- charls %>%
  filter(hibpe_wave1 == 0) %>%  # No hypertension at baseline
  select(ID, age_wave1, gender_wave1, rural_wave1,
         bmi_wave1, smokev_wave1,
         hibpe_wave1, hibpe_wave2, hibpe_wave3, hibpe_wave4, hibpe_wave5) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("hibpe_wave"),
    names_to = "wave",
    names_pattern = "hibpe_wave(.*)",
    values_to = "hypertension"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    wave = as.numeric(wave),
    gender = factor(gender_wave1, labels = c("Male", "Female")),
    rural  = factor(rural_wave1, labels = c("Urban", "Rural")),
    smoke  = factor(smokev_wave1, labels = c("Never", "Former", "Current")),
    time = wave - 1  # Years since baseline (biennial waves)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(hypertension))

# GEE with exchangeable correlation structure
gee_fit <- geeglm(
  hypertension ~ time + age_wave1 + gender + rural + bmi_wave1 + smoke,
  id = ID, data = htn_long, family = binomial,
  corstr = "exchangeable"
)

summary(gee_fit)

#' ---
#' ## Example 4: Frailty Index Construction (Wave 1)
#'
#' Demonstrating how to build a deficit-accumulation frailty index
#' from harmonized variables (available in the extended dataset).

# (This example uses extended variables available in the full harmonized dataset.
#  Requires additional ADL, IADL, and chronic disease items.)
#
# frailty_items <- c(
#   "adl_dressing_wave1", "adl_bathing_wave1", "adl_eating_wave1",
#   "iadl_shopping_wave1", "iadl_cooking_wave1",
#   "hibpe_wave1", "diabe_wave1", "hearte_wave1", "stroke_wave1",
#   "arthre_wave1", "lunge_wave1", "cancre_wave1",
#   "cesd10_wave1", "total_cognition_wave1",
#   "bmi_wave1", "sleep_wave1"
# )
#
# fi_data <- charls[, frailty_items]
# fi_score <- rowMeans(fi_data, na.rm = TRUE)  # 0–1 scale

cat("\nVignette complete. See manuscript for full analysis details.\n")
